entries.title: The FCA Is Building a Sterling Stablecoin Market. The Rules That Kick In When It Succeeds Could Kill It.

BREAKING: The White House is now reviewing the SEC's crypto regulatory framework, signaling potential policy shifts ahead.

This could reshape how traditional finance institutions approach digital asset compliance and market entry.

entries.title: U.S. Regulators Advancing Oversight of Crypto and Prediction Markets; SEC Submits Guidance on Securities Laws Application
entries.snippet: Wu Blockchain (@WuBlockchain): According to Bloomberg, U.S. regulators are advancing oversight of crypto and prediction markets. The U.S. SEC has submitted commission-level guidance to the White House on the application of federal securities laws to certain crypto assets, potentially introducing a t
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: The SEC is submitting formal guidance to the White House on how federal securities laws apply to crypto assets and prediction markets.

Watch for potential regulatory framework that could reshape how digital assets are classified and traded.

entries.title: Zerohash seeks national trust bank charter from OCC
entries.snippet: The firm wants to be able to provide services like custody over digital assets, fiat currency, and other assets.

entries.title: Western Union teams with Crossmint to support USDPT stablecoin on Solana
entries.snippet: The infrastructure provider will support the planned launch of Western Union’s USDPT stablecoin on Solana, linking blockchain payments to its global payout network.

entries.title: Canada's Scotiabank launches multi-crypto ETF with 3iQ
entries.snippet: The bank's asset manager and 3iQ debut an actively managed crypto ETF to Canadian investors, offering exposure to Bitcoin, Ether, Solana and XRP at a competitive 0.25% fee.

entries.title: Sui's native stablecoin goes live with promise of Treasury yield going back to the network
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: Sui launches native stablecoin with Treasury yield flowing back to the network—a novel mechanism few protocols have attempted.

Watch whether this sustainable yield model becomes the template competitors must match.

entries.title: MEXC Partners with Ondo Finance to Launch Tokenized US Equities in Defense and Energy Sectors

entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: @MEXC_Official and @OndoFinance tokenize US equities in defense and energy sectors—bringing institutional-grade RWA products to crypto traders.

What happens when regulated tokenized securities hit mainstream exchanges? Market structure could shift fast.

entries.title: Kraken wins Kansas City Fed approval for limited master account access
entries.snippet: In a first for crypto, Kraken’s banking unit gained access to the US Federal Reserve’s payment system, Fedwire, though without full banking privileges such as interest on reserves.
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: @kaboracle's banking unit just gained access to the Federal Reserve's payment system—a historic first for crypto.

This legitimizes digital asset infrastructure and signals regulators are warming to institutional crypto integration.

entries.title: Standard Chartered appointed as custodian for TP ICAP's Fusion Digital Assets
entries.snippet: Standard Chartered has been appointed as the digital asset custodian and settlement agent for TP ICAP’s Fusion Digital Assets. This partnership will support the global markets infrastructure provider as it expands its matched principal trading activities.

Since its launch in December 2025, Predict fun has recorded $1.5 billion in cumulative trading volu
entries.tweet_text: Predict.fun's $1.5B trading volume in under a year signals how prediction markets are consolidating around leading platforms. The acquisition of Probable strengthens BNB Chain's position as infrastructure for decentralized betting and derivatives.

entries.title: Network International powers card tokenization for Apple smartphones in Egypt

entries.title: Stablecoins could pose risk to monetary policy
entries.snippet: The increasing popularity of stablecoins could lead to a decline in retail deposits for banks in the eurozone and weaken the effectiveness of monetary policy, according to a European Central Bank study.
